task_objective: Land the lunar module safely between the flags, centered, slow, upright, fuel-efficient, and end with stable ground contact.

phase_structure.purpose: encourage measurable progress toward landing zone by descending and centering
phase_structure.reward_intent: Reward lower y (closer to ground) and smaller |x|, while already penalizing excessive speed and tilt to avoid crashing.

phase_structure.purpose: encourage extra caution when near ground to ensure soft touchdown
phase_structure.reward_intent: Conditional penalty on velocity and tilt when y is small, emphasising gentle final descent.

phase_structure.purpose: grant a large one‑time bonus for a stable landing
phase_structure.reward_intent: Trigger when both legs are in contact and velocities/angle are within safe bounds; also penalise a hard crash.

component_blueprint.name: r_progress_guidance
component_blueprint.design_intent: Creates a shaped reward that increases as the lander descends (lower y) and stays centered (x≈0), while continuous penalties on speed and tilt prevent the agent from simply crashing down.
component_blueprint.anti_exploit_note: The -y term gives a monotonically higher reward as altitude decreases; the agent cannot exploit this by hovering (y stays high, reward stays low) or by crashing (large vy/vx/angle penalties dominate). Thus dense reward is aligned with safe progress.

component_blueprint.name: r_near_ground_quality
component_blueprint.design_intent: Adds extra penalty on velocity and tilt only when the lander is close to the ground, encouraging a very soft final segment.
component_blueprint.anti_exploit_note: This component is inactive while y is large, so the agent cannot accumulate easy reward earlier by hovering low; it only tightens landing quality when touchdown is imminent.

component_blueprint.name: r_control_cost
component_blueprint.design_intent: Penalizes fuel consumption to encourage efficient use of the main and side engines.
component_blueprint.anti_exploit_note: main_engine is non‑negative, side_engine is signed; using abs(side_engine) ensures any lateral thrust costs fuel. The formula is reward‑signed (negative), so it always reduces total reward and cannot be exploited to create positive reward.

anti_exploit_principles: Dense reward should not be maximized indefinitely without progress (e.g., by hovering) because -y remains low at high altitude.
Stability/alignment penalties are present globally but increase near ground, so they support progress rather than replace it.
Action cost uses abs(side_engine) and penalizes both directions equally, preventing any signed action from accidentally becoming a positive reward.
Terminal success is sparse and one‑time, based only on primitive terminal evidence, so the agent cannot repeatedly collect it.
